January 9, 1990

*PROFESSIONAL CORPORATION
TA PROFESSIONAL ASSOCIATION
ADMITTED KANSAS & MISSOURI
*ADMITTED KANSAS

COUNSEL:
DWIGHT A. HEWELL
MARY REDUS GAYLE*

WRITER'S DIRECT DIAL
213-236-2806
OUR FILE NO. 00396-001

Margaret W. Ellison, Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 "J" Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95840-0807

Re: City of West Covina/Mass Mailing Provisions

Dear Ms. Ellison:

Pursuant to our conversation of January 2, 1990, I
am now requesting written advice from the Fair Political
Practices Commission pursuant to Section 83114(b) of the
Political Reform Act regarding the mass mailing provisions
of the Political Reform Act (Section 89001 et seq. of the
Government Code). This letter is being written on behalf of
the City of West Covina for which this office is the City
Attorney. I will briefly outline the factual circumstances.

The City of West Covina ("City") has entered into
an agreement with Mt. San Antonio College ("College") and
Cencom Cable Television ("Cencom"). This agreement has
created an entity entitled MSAC Cablevision. Cencom has a
monopoly franchise for the operation and promotion of
cable television within the City's boundaries. MSAC
Cablevision provides programming on a community access
channel seen over Cencom pursuant to the franchise
agreement.

Margaret W. Ellison, Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 9, 1990
Page 2


The agreement also has created a Cable
Communications Cooperative Board, Public Access
Subcommittee and an Education Access Subcommittee as
advisory bodies to MSAC Cablevision. Of these three named
boards, the City has one staff member on the Cooperative
Board. The City also contributes a portion of MSAC
Cablevision's yearly budget.

All of the employees of MSAC Cablevision are
employees of the College. In addition, these employees are
solely responsible for the printing of MSAC Cablevision's
program guide which promotes programming on the community
access channel only. In fact, all of MSAC Cablevision's
bulk mail is run through a bulk mail permit of the
College. Subscriptions to cable service are made through
Cencom only. The City has no responsibility nor input
in such advertising or for obtaining new subscribers. In
addition, the City has no input as to the contents of MSAC
Cablevision's program guide. Since Cencom is the only cable
franchise within the City's boundaries, any resident of West
Covina wishing to obtain cable television must subscribe to
Cencom.

Cencom shall be televising the City Council
meetings of the City of West Covina on the public access
channel under the franchise agreement. MSAC Cablevision
desires to advertise in its program guide that such meetings
shall be televised to the residents of West Covina. MSAC
Cablevision has asked the permission of the City Council to
use a picture of the City Council in its program guide. It
is our belief that this picture shall be used for
advertising purposes only and to notify the residents of
West Covina that such meetings shall be televised. The
councilmembers names shall be used with the pictures.

Before the City allows the Council's picture and/or
names to appear in MSAC Cablevision's program guide, the
City has asked this office to determine whether the use of a
picture of the City Council would violate the mass mailings
provisions of the Political Reform Act. If the picture of
the Council is violative of the Act, the City would also
like to know whether the use of the councilmembers' names
alone would be similarly violative of the Act. This office
is especially interested in the applicability of an FPPC

Margaret W. Ellison, Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
January 9, 1990
Page 3


advice letter written to Judith A. Harper dated September
21, 1989 (your file number I-89-435) in which the staff
found that the inclusion of a photograph of elected school
board members in school yearbooks would not violate the mass
mailing prohibitions of the Political Reform Act. It is our
belief that the situation of the City is sufficiently not
analogous to find that the city's conduct is similarly not
violative of the mass mailing provisions of the Political
Reform Act.

     If you have any other questions, or need other
factual details, please feel free to contact me. Thank you
for your courtesy and cooperation.

                              very truly yours,

                              [signature]
                              JAMES R. FELTON
                              for BURKE, WILLIAMS & SORENSEN

JF/rc
jfz/LTR43265
